item.title: “Patience Pays Off” (a gallery of images that took a long time to obtain)
item.description:
After three years of observing celestial patterns and tracking the satellite’s position, Brazilian photographer Leonardo Sens was finally able to take this breathtaking photo of Christ the Redeemer “holding” the Moon.
In December 2023, Italian photographer Valerio Minato captured an astonishing shot of the Basilica of Superga and Monviso Mountain perfectly aligned with the Moon. After six years of failed attempts, he finally achieved the shot he had been envisioning since 2017.
It took six years, 4,200 hours, and 720,000 photos for wildlife photographer Alan McFadyen to capture this perfect shot of a kingfisher diving into the water, a tribute to his late grandfather.
It took ten years for the Italian astrophotographer Marcella Julia Pace to capture these forty-eight colors of the Moon.
Titled “Bruce the Bald Eagle,” this photograph is the result of Canadian photographer Steve Biro’s ten-year quest for the perfectly symmetrical shot.
It took numerous close encounters with squirrels throughout his entire career for Vadim Trunov to capture these adorable images of squirrels and birds photographing each other.
Cape Buffalo and Red-billed Oxpecker take a drink together by Clint Ralph. “The bottom line is to show up with the right equipment, lots of patience and a bit of luck and hopefully end the day with a few nice shots.”
Photographer Louis-Marie Preau went underwater for four years — night after night in snorkeling gear and weights — to take a photograph of a beaver delivering a branch to its family in the Loire River.
Jon Carmichael spent years trying to figure out the best way to get a shot of the solar eclipse. In 2017 he took a flight from Portland to St. Louis and captured this image at 39,000 feet. He spent hundreds of hours over the course of a year editing more than 1,200 photos.
Photographer Mario Cea Sanchez took this perfect picture after 6 months of failed attempts and over 2000 shots. The kingfisher visited this pond every day, and Mario used a high shutter speed with artificial light to photograph it. This image does not use photoshop.
It took French photographer Laurent Ballesta five years to capture this striking photo showing the otherworldly beauty of spawning groupers. This phenomenon occurs only once a year, around the full moon in July.
Clint Ralph visited the same waterhole for four years waiting for this shot.
It took eleven months for photographer Sergey Gorshkov to capture his award-winning image of a Siberian tiger hugging an ancient Manchurian fir in eastern Russia.
Tampa photographer Jean Claude spent three years attempting to capture the perfect sunset photo at Clearwater Pier, Florida. When he finally managed to take the shot, a bird flew into the frame, adding an extra touch of beauty.
In 1999, photographer Thomas D. Mangelsen waited forty-two days to capture a photo of a cougar, spending twelve to fourteen hours on location daily, with breaks to sleep at home. He finally got the shot of the female cougar at dusk emerging from her den. The shot was among the first photographs to document the life of a wild cougar and it helped launch a movement to protect the cats against humans invading their habitat.
Italian astrophotographer Giorgia Hofer created this composite photo of the positions and phases of the Moon by taking a picture from the same location each day for twenty-eight days.
The Tadami Line in winter by Kameyama Takeshi. “When I decided to focus on capturing the snowy landscape with the Tadami Line train in Fukushima, I would wake up at 2 a.m., drive for three hours on snowy roads, climb a pitch-black mountain path, and wait in sub-zero temperatures for about an hour until the train arrived. This process was repeated time and again.”
It took Jack Zhi four years to capture the rare sight of a female falcon attacking a large brown pelican.
Italian photographer Ciro Russo captured the sunset over the tower at the Castelfiorentino archaeological site every month for a year, creating a stunning “visual diary.”
Photographer Mithun Hunugund waited six days to capture this photo of a leopard and her “black panther shadow” in the wild. “Saaya and Cleopatra have been courting since four years now and whenever they are together it’s a sight to behold.”
Stefano Zanarello positioned himself approximately 1,300 meters from the Arc de Triomphe in Paris, waiting for the Moon to travel across the cloudless night sky and align perfectly with the neoclassical monument.
Photographer Loes Heerink transformed the ordinary into a stunning collection by waking up at 4 a.m. and positioning herself on various bridges around Hanoi, patiently waiting for the passage of street vendors. “I once spent five hours in a location with no shots,” Heerink explained. “This project started my fascination for the women carrying their goods. They have no clue how beautiful their bicycles are, no idea they create little pieces of art every day.”
Varun Aditya captured this stunning clip of a pride of lions without using a flash. The photographer spent three nights in a hiding place, waiting for the perfect moment.
Photographer Terry Donnelly spent a year coaxing red squirrels into his garden to capture stunning photos of them mid-jump and playing with various minuscule musical instruments. https://x.com/JamesLucasIT/status/1799856071609049548 I hope you enjoyed this respite from ::gestures to the world at large:: everything!
item.dc:creator: KalliJ

item.title: If you make a nostril sound, you have to hit the green arrow thing MEME DUMP
item.description:
1. Hahha this guy is great, and wasn't being a dick
2. Subtle
3
4
5
6. Get it grandma (someone put an appropriate beat in the comments)
7. Where my Sea of Thieves homies at?
8. EMBRACE THE CHONK
9
10. Japan explain this
11. All of us here madly upvoting non political posts
12. Average day in America
13. For a few seconds I thought it was real, then I thought it was CGI.
14. "Never should've come here"
15.Thanks for getting to the end of the dump, hope this dump made your nostril move at least once! I’m happy to share that I've been working on my dream game for the past year and a half, and it’s almost here! "Do Not Press The Button (To Delete The Multiverse)" is a quirky adventure that my brother and I have poured our hearts into. Your support means the world to us! Adding our game to your wishlist helps us reach more gamers and keeps you updated on the release. Thank you for helping us make this dream come true. Let's save the multiverse together! https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/

item.title: These are true friends.
item.description: So respectful. Full video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=h2nWM4iukR4 "My name is jarom ngakuru im born and rasied in New Zealad but live in Texas USA. I received news that my grandmother had just passed away and I couldn’t make it back to say my goodbyes so my boys at work got together without me knowing and made a card that they all signed and learned how to do a haka to show their love and appreciation. I couldn’t make it home so they ended up bringing home to me. ??"

As usual, please pause to read text if you're interested in the extra explanations. I will edit all the days into one big longer Youtube video once I'm done with the project :) Pics will be posted here : https://www.instagram.com/yuzu.pyon Anyways, going to buy more materials today and start Day 4 of the project! (+ edit Day 3 to post later) Wishing you a great day, YuzuPyon

item.title: The word "Hero" has lost all meaning in this society. The world is overrun by fakes and criminals who chase petty dreams. They must all be purged. Everything I do is to create a stronger society.
item.description:
The media portrays heroes as those who make sacrifices for others. In war, it's the soldiers, in a pandemic, it's nurses and essential workers. But you would think that if our actions are so heroic, we should be better compensated, right? Those soldiers that are wounded and come home, should be given a hero's welcome, right? Everything taken care of for them, because they did their duty and managed to return home. But, how many GOOD stories have you heard about how the VA treats our wounded vets? None come to my mind. The nurses and essential workers, they worked through YEARS of pandemic, years of unknown factors, constant fighting against misinformation, idiots ignoring the regulations and even becoming belligerent over them, sometimes for something as simple and stupid as a haircut. But once the pandemic passed, and we asked to look at better wages, where did that word "Hero" go? Suddenly we were entitled shits, lucky to have jobs at all. Remote work no longer allowed, forced to go back to the pointless office. The media and politicians love a hero, but we no longer fit the description to them, and there's a good reason as to why. Heroes DIE. It's easy to honor a dead hero when they aren't alive to speak against you. You can say that hero stood for anything you support, and that hero can't say otherwise. But we lived, we spoke out, and now we're not what they deem a hero.
They want dead heroes, because they're much quieter.
This is one of, if not the biggest, reason I don't have faith in humanity, and no amount of people saving cats can ever restore it. You wanna know why my username never seems to fit? Because it's a mask, it's who I need to be. It's the opposite of what I AM.
item.dc:creator: Onlyhereforthelaughs

item.title: Sean Lock - RIP. You never gave a fook.
item.description: Some people who react to this stuff on YT say this was scripted. It wasn't. I wasn't working for C4 at this time but I was in media production. It's about 25% scripted. The basic set up of a story is scripted. But that is all. Then everything the comedians say is pure improvisation. No one could have predicated Sean's remark. Sadly Missed.
item.dc:creator: MissYotsuba

item.title: Even more freshly stolen Trans MEmes cause I'm bored and don't feel like playing Nightingale tonight.
item.description:
Seriously, I can't speak for everyone who is Trans but I would not wish being Trans on anyone, not even myself and it's a sentiment I see echoed constantly in Trans spaces. The phrase "I don't want to be trans I just..." Until you accept your inner self it's a painful existence and even after you do it can take years to finally look the way you feel and even then you may still get misgendered, chased or attacked.
The button meme for most is like win a jillion dollars but something else good/bad in the world. Or every time you press the button X thing happens but Y thing happens too and the guy is just banging the button forever... For most Trans though the button meme is almost always Be in preferred genders body and for some even just Be Cis.
One Day...
It's ok to be you. Unless you're a bigot then you need to re-evaluate your life.
NGL (I know this is a sweater but) I kinda want a hoodie like that now.
I was able to steal a few good ones for the Boyo's today. I'll be sprinkling them here and there.
I think my Spiro dosage is too low for the salt/pickle cravings yet... I do crave Munchos for some reason though and can't seem to find em recently.
Take it all, it's literally poison.
Don't care what Gender/presentation/Sexuality you are, don't care if you want a social, medical or surgical transition or all the above. You. Are. Valid. and don't let anyone tell you otherwise.
Artist is NottAMimic on Deviant art and Reddit.
SLUUUUUUUURRRRRRRP... I haven't had monster in months cuz I'm trying to control my health... I'm still slipping on the food side though ;_;
The Irony.
New Trans Icon? (From Monster Hunter Wilds trailer)
I'm Canadian (and I don't think I'm Autistic but who knows? ) but I'm pretty sure Reagan fucked some shit for us too considering how our Conservatives have a massive boner for shitting on the people, selling off assets to private equity and sucking Corpo Dick.
A feel for you and a fear what happens here if PeePee and his conservatives get elected up here...
Really wish we could go back in time and erase Cass from existence. Her Bullshit is actively harming kids under the guise of protecting them and other countries are looking at it salivating.
I'm willing to bet he's a closeted chaser.
We can share things, we can be concerned about multiple things at once. Multitasking is a thing. You can't have my E Patches though... I need those firmware updates.
How it feels like arguing with "Centrist" and Transphobes sometimes. I really should stop doing it...
If I could give mine to someone I would but if you're masc, store bought is fine.
This one is more for Mascs but I still haven't told my folks yet. I sometimes wonder if I ever showed signs to them but I was pretty closed off so I doubt it.
She's becoming a bit of an Icon now and I'm all for it.
I really need to train but I'd like to have more of an Ara Ara voice. (my fellow weebs will know what I means :P )
Few weeks ago while chatting with a coworker in her office after work, she looked at the time and called out to our boss to see if he was almost finished with his work as he was her lift home. Was only Her, Me and Him in the office. Boss replied "I need 2 more minutes. Are you girls ready to leave?" I'm not femme presenting at all yet but I am out to both of them. 5 hours later and I'm pretty sure you still could not have jackhammered the smile off my face.
Pretty sure Gandalf would be an Ally but this made me giggle.
It is a pretty badass design tbh and I'm glad we stole both it and the rainbow.
As a baby Trans every time I post or reply to others posts, ya'll are always so sweet, offering advice, encouragement, assuaging concerns, giving support and DM invites and it's just nice to see. Makes me want to return the favor and the pass love along.
This concludes this round of meme pilfering. Raids will continue until head pats and snacks are aquired.
item.dc:creator: MelodyLightfoot

item.title: The Ancient Texts pt-15
item.description:
Phone Clearing meme bump circa June 2019 I was a different person back then babe I swear!
Save some puss for the rest of us damn. I joke but I also had a balloon animal making phase. It's kinda a fun hobby and you get to meet a lot of cute clowns.
Know the difference. Report puppy mills.
A dozen Nathan Pyle comics for your pleasure.
Learning real life lessons instead. Like how anyone can come in and bet a dollar just fucking up your hard earned work. Life is cruel.
*builds another arena for no reason*
Why can I hear this image?
We all return to the hole one day.
As a younger sibling ouch. 3real5me
I specifically saved this to send to someone named Jennifer. Please continue the ritual.
Pre COVID antivaxx memes have such a funny but somber vibe to me. We had no idea how wild people would get.
Captain Canuck is taking the picture.
I'm an engineer and sometimes tradespeople will ask me something and I just lose 300 IQ immediately. I just want the millwrights to think I'm cool man...
Pop quiz, what's the average value across the games for a green rupee.
Still beautiful.
That was fun. Ok thanks byyeeeeee

item.dc:creator: tractorist

item.title: "Pro life" Texas almost kills another woman
item.description: Texas radio host Ryan Hamilton says that after his wife had a miscarriage at 13 weeks — and there was no fetal heartbeat — she was denied proper medical treatment. On May 16, she called him and “she said our baby has no heartbeat, and that’s all that she could really get out,” Hamilton told CBS Mornings on Tuesday. She first visited an emergency center in North Texas, the outlet reported, where doctors confirmed the fetus didn’t have a heartbeat. His wife was prescribed misoprostol — commonly called an “abortion drug” although it’s used for both miscarriages and abortions — to “finish the process of what had already started at home,” Hamilton told CBS. His wife was sent home but after two days, she hadn’t expelled the nonviable fetus. Since she had been told to return to the emergency center if she needed to repeat the medication, she went back. However, this time, Hamilton says the doctor told them, “Due to the current stance, I cannot prescribe this medicine for you.” Hamilton explained his frustration with the seemingly inconsistent medical practices. “There’s no explanation from them, so you just assume the stance of the State of Texas, because of the law,” he told CBS. Although the law allows exceptions for medical emergencies, Hamilton says he was told “it was not enough of an emergency to perform a D&C,” short for dilation & curettage, a procedure where tissue inside the uterus is removed. That’s where she called him from the bathroom, and Hamilton said found her on the floor, unconscious, with a trail of blood leading from the toilet. He said when they returned to the hospital, he was told she could have died. https://people.com/ryan-hamilton-wife-miscarriage-texas-radio-host-abortion-law-8657876
item.dc:creator: stephlin15

item.title: Squirrel receives a new harness
item.description: In this video, we observe a squirrel having to put down its treat, only to receive a harness from its human friend. Hope Monday goes easy for you all. FP EDIT: Hope Monday has not been too rough in ya! Does anybody have any idea how to make car badges? Or vinal stickers. I need to come up with a "1.8 Squirrel Powered " I just dunno where to start for the badge.
item.dc:creator: AnSquirrel
